notes:
Plaintiff opening work notes for arb-ex06-20260630005431.

Opportunity: openings:plaintiff. Allowed final action: record_opening_statement only. Evidence admission blocked. Record inspection required and completed.

Current record/evidence: get_case shows empty record; list_evidence shows no visible evidence. No case-packet files available to stat/read.

Issue map: Plaintiff must prove U.S. Armed Forces, ground forces, entered Venezuelan sovereign territory, combat capacity, and objective of seizing/holding territory, removing sitting government, or establishing operational control of a major population center. Primary source hierarchy is DoD/War Department or White House official statement; UN Security Council records matter if official U.S. sources conflict. Expiration Jan. 31, 2026.

Adverse/limiting issues: Proposition expressly resolves NO for targeted strikes, naval blockades, cyber operations, and special operations raids without territorial objectives. Edge case says capture of head of state without territorial occupation is NO. Therefore capture of Maduro alone will not carry plaintiff unless tied to ground entry plus territorial/major-population-center control or a qualifying government-removal/operational-control objective.

Native investigation performed before opening: targeted web searches for official DoD/White House/UN materials. Leads located for later evidence phase include official War.gov article titled 'Trump Announces U.S. Military's Capture of Maduro' at https://www.war.gov/News/News-Stories/Article/Article/4370431/trump-announces-us-militarys-capture-of-maduro/; White House SAP PDF on S.J. Res. 98 at https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/SAP-SJR98.pdf; White House fact sheet on Venezuelan oil revenue at https://www.whitehouse.gov/fact-sheets/2026/01/fact-sheet-president-donald-j-trump-safeguards-venezuelan-oil-revenue-for-the-good-of-the-american-and-venezuelan-people/; and UN/Security Council lead for Jan. 2026 Venezuela emergency meeting records. These are not admitted and will not be cited as record proof in opening.

Planned opening theory: Be candid that the starting record is empty. Frame the real dispute as whether the operation was merely a nonqualifying capture/raid or whether official statements show ground entry in combat capacity with a territorial/control/removal objective. Ask council to focus on source hierarchy, objective, physical presence, and edge-case carveout. Preserve plaintiff position that if official statements establish ground entry into Venezuelan territory with operational control of Caracas or governmental-removal/territorial objectives, the proposition resolves YES by preponderance.

Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Opening opportunity: defendant, `openings:defendant`; final act allowed `record_opening_statement`; evidence submission not allowed.

Record/evidence inspection: `get_case` shows only plaintiff opening. `list_evidence` returns empty list; no case packet files, exhibits, submitted evidence, or evidence metadata visible. No `stat_evidence`/`read_evidence_range` possible because there are no evidence IDs.

Issue map: YES requires official-source proof that U.S. ground troops entered Venezuelan sovereign territory in combat capacity with the objective of seizing/holding territory, removing sitting government, or establishing operational control of a major population center. Express NO carveouts include targeted strikes, naval blockades, special operations raids without territorial objectives, cyber operations, and capture of a head of state without territorial occupation. Expiration Jan. 31, 2026.

Defense theory: plaintiff must prove more than armed presence or a capture/extraction. Even if later admitted official sources show a brief U.S. ground raid inside Caracas, the proposition excludes a head-of-state capture without territorial occupation and excludes special operations raids without territorial objectives. The defense should force plaintiff to show the qualifying objective from admitted official sources, not rhetoric.

Targeted investigation before opening: searched official-source domains. Leads found: Defense/War Department article titled “Trump Announces U.S. Military's Capture of Maduro” (Jan. 3, 2026) at defense.gov/war.gov; White House release “RUBIO: This Is Our Hemisphere...” (Jan. 4, 2026); UN Security Council coverage `press.un.org/en/2026/sc16271.doc.htm`; Defense transcript of Hegseth remarks at Newport News Shipyard. Observed leads appear adverse in part (ground-based extraction force in Caracas; nearly 200 Americans in downtown Caracas; Trump language about Maduro out of power/U.S. overseeing Venezuela) and favorable in part (Rubio says forces were on ground about two hours to capture Maduro, no current ground forces, not a war, not an invasion, not an extended military operation; Hegseth frames as grabbing an indicted individual wanted by American justice in support of law enforcement).

Opening discipline: because the current record contains no admitted evidence, opening should not claim those leads as proof. It should identify them as the kind of official materials the later evidence phase must preserve and test. Later evidence plan: submit official source captures; check exact wording and provenance; compare DoD/White House/UN for conflict; test whether “oversee Venezuela” language proves operational control/removal objective or merely post-raid political rhetoric; assess succession by Delcy Rodriguez as a missing/limiting link.

Plaintiff merits work notes for arb-ex06-20260630005431.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Allowed final action: submit_argument. Evidence admission allowed and used.

Record/evidence pre-check: get_case showed plaintiff and defendant openings only, no evidence. list_evidence initially empty. After submissions, list_evidence/stat_evidence confirmed three plaintiff evidence items, all text-readable.

Evidence submitted and checked:
1. ev_e2e3a57baa70_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-e2e3a57baa70, Department of War article, SHA-256 e2e3a57baa70e69f9927304d9c9a91a4794abe0734394c8c42f772ddb322b0b3, size 6363. War.gov direct curl returned HTTP 403 even with browser UA; web.open rendered official page text. Key contents: Jan. 3, 2026 Pentagon News article says Trump announced capture of Maduro after overnight joint U.S. military extraction in Caracas; operation in capital/heart of Caracas; joint forces including special operations; integration of air, ground, space, maritime operations; 150 aircraft providing cover for ground-based extraction force in Caracas; Maduro out of power; U.S. will oversee Venezuela until transition.
2. ev_c0374ffa33d5_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-c0374ffa33d5, White House/OMB SAP on S.J. Res. 98, SHA-256 c0374ffa33d52a5f1d3a47df3d91b330310833bb7b07f58337a3002632f4a1a4, size 3198. PDF downloaded locally size 159669, sha256 ee2cf252a56c4c345e913bc8dde77640b6b930e095b7488d1906d52ec4b70678. pdftotext/file/strings/pip unavailable, so web.open PDF text used. Key/adverse content: targeted and limited military strikes within Venezuela in furtherance of law enforcement operation to apprehend/transport Maduro and Flores; U.S. Armed Forces necessary because location beyond ordinary law enforcement reach, protected by combat-equipped armed associates, anticipated resistance.
3. ev_5bc8b01e9699_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-5bc8b01e9699, White House fact sheet, SHA-256 5bc8b01e969901f09457e11f8845509143cfadf3938b4511a2855855041e676b, size 3246. HTML fetched locally and text extracted with Python HTMLParser. Supports broader U.S. objective/control context: safeguards Venezuelan oil revenue in U.S. Treasury custody, U.S. objectives for political/economic stability, states last week U.S. armed forces conducted extraordinary military operation in support of law enforcement.

Adverse checks and gaps: Defense will rely on SAP language calling the operation targeted/limited and law-enforcement-related, plus proposition edge case that capture of a head of state without territorial occupation is NO. Plaintiff response: DOW article supplies more than capture: ground-based extraction force in Caracas, operation in capital/fortress, Maduro out of power, U.S. oversight of Venezuela until transition. UN press release lead https://press.un.org/en/2026/sc16271.doc.htm found but local fetch returned client challenge; not submitted. UN only becomes binding if DoD/White House conflict. Full press conference transcript/video remains useful but not necessary for present preponderance argument.

Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Arguments opportunity: defendant, `arguments:defendant`; final act allowed `submit_argument`; evidence submission allowed.

Record/evidence scan completed with `get_case`, `list_evidence`, `stat_evidence`, and `read_evidence_range`. Plaintiff submitted PX-1 Department of War article (`ev_e2e3a57baa70...`, SHA-256 e2e3a57...), PX-2 White House OMB SAP (`ev_c0374ffa33d5...`, SHA-256 c0374...), and PX-3 White House oil-revenue fact sheet (`ev_5bc8b01e9699...`, SHA-256 5bc8...). Exact text read for all three.

Defense evidence submitted and accepted: `ev_221f925d2d25_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-221f925d2d25`, White House Rubio release, SHA-256 221f925..., proving no current U.S. forces on ground, ground presence about two hours to capture Maduro, not a war against Venezuela, not an invasion, not an extended military operation, and describing the event as raid/capture/arrest. `ev_96090e61aecd_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-96090e61aecd`, Department of War Hegseth transcript, SHA-256 96090..., proving nearly 200 Americans went downtown Caracas and grabbed an indicted individual wanted by American justice in support of law enforcement.

Adverse analysis: PX-1 strongly proves physical entry and combat-capable ground raid in Caracas and contains adverse language that Maduro was out of power and that the U.S. would oversee Venezuela until transition. PX-2 is a high-value limiting White House source: targeted and limited military strikes within Venezuela, in furtherance of law-enforcement apprehension and transportation to the United States for criminal prosecution; U.S. Armed Forces necessary to effectuate apprehension/transport due to fortified location and armed associates. PX-3 is post-operation oil revenue policy and says extraordinary military operation in support of law enforcement, but does not establish ground occupation or operational control. DX-1 and DX-2 reinforce limited-duration raid/capture/law-enforcement framing. UN press-release lead `press.un.org/en/2026/sc16271.doc.htm` was attempted via web.open but yielded no usable content in this turn, and plaintiff did not submit UN text; no admitted UN record conflicts with U.S. sources.

Defense filing theory: concede physical entry/combat capacity only insofar as record proves it; argue plaintiff fails qualifying objective. Proposition specifically says capture of a head of state without territorial occupation resolves NO, and special operations raids without territorial objectives resolve NO. Record describes capture/apprehension/transport/arrest/prosecution and brief ground presence; no admitted source proves seizing or holding territory, operational control of Caracas, or a ground-troop objective to remove/replace the government. PX-1's political aftermath language is ambiguous and weaker than PX-2/DX-1/DX-2 because it does not tie oversight/transition to ground troops or territorial occupation; PX-1 also notes Rodriguez was sworn in, undermining a claim that the U.S. established the government by military control.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes for arb-ex06-20260630005431.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Record/evidence check completed with get_case and list_evidence. Defendant added two evidence items: ev_221f925d2d25_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-221f925d2d25 (DX-1, White House Rubio release, SHA-256 221f925d2d2559b8018a3ae816c962764f01458115a11cec9f9a05c2294e3d0b, size 1704) and ev_96090e61aecd_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-96090e61aecd (DX-2, DOW Hegseth transcript, SHA-256 96090e61aecd9302e180f8aa97ac3e1a506fcfbdad7df0dc107129a384b3d88f, size 956). Used read_evidence_range on both items to verify exact text.

Defendant strongest point: Rubio says no war against Venezuela, no current U.S. forces on ground, forces were on ground about two hours to capture Maduro, not an invasion, not an extended military operation. Hegseth says nearly 200 Americans went downtown Caracas and grabbed an indicted individual in support of law enforcement. Defense argues this triggers capture/raid NO edge case.

Plaintiff rebuttal analysis: DX-1 and DX-2 are also concessions: U.S. forces physically on ground in Venezuela, about two hours; nearly 200 Americans went downtown Caracas; this reinforces ground entry in a major population center and combat-capable context. Proposition does not require invasion or extended operation. Edge case bars capture without territorial occupation/control objective; it does not bar an operation that includes removal from power and U.S. oversight/transition control. PX-1 remains the only source addressing what followed: Maduro out of power, U.S. overseeing Venezuela until safe transition, and U.S. officials directing what was necessary. DX-1 statement retaining all options until changes are made fits coercive/control posture rather than purely completed arrest.

No new external evidence needed. Plan: focused rebuttal, offer DX-1/DX-2 as plaintiff rebuttal exhibits because they directly answer defendant and contain admissions. Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Surrebuttal opportunity: defendant, `surrebuttals:defendant`; allowed final acts `submit_surrebuttal` or pass; evidence submission allowed but no new evidence appears necessary.

Record/evidence scan: `get_case` and `list_evidence` show no new source evidence submitted by plaintiff in rebuttal. Evidence list remains PX-1/PX-2/PX-3 plus defense DX-1/DX-2. Plaintiff rebuttal offered/used defense exhibits as PX-R1/PX-R2 and did not submit new source content. No need for broader outside investigation because rebuttal turns on legal/evidentiary interpretation of existing record.

Plaintiff rebuttal theory: defense supposedly rewrites proposition to require invasion, extended occupation, or permanent troop presence; DX-1/DX-2 concede ground entry and combat capacity; edge case does not make every brief capture automatically NO; PX-1's "out of power," "U.S. will oversee Venezuela," and working with successor prove removal/control objective.

Defense surrebuttal theory: accept that entry/combat are not the core dispute; objective remains dispositive. Defense does not require permanent occupation; it requires the proposition's own extra fact beyond head-of-state capture: territorial occupation, holding territory, operational control of a major population center, or a qualifying military objective to remove the government. Plaintiff's theory makes the edge case meaningless because every successful capture of a head of state will tend to leave that person out of power. PX-2 states the purpose was apprehension/transport for prosecution; DX-1 says about two hours, not an invasion, not an extended operation, raid/capture/arrest; DX-2 says grabbing an indicted individual in support of law enforcement. PX-1's oversight/transition language is post-capture political or diplomatic language not tied to ground troops remaining or controlling Caracas; Rodriguez sworn in points away from U.S. operational control. PX-3 oil revenue custody is remote financial/diplomatic leverage, not ground control.

Plaintiff closing work notes for arb-ex06-20260630005431.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Final action: deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence admission or technical reports. Final record/evidence scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Record now contains plaintiff merits, defendant merits, plaintiff rebuttal, defendant surrebuttal, five visible text-readable evidence items, and technical reports. No new post-surrebuttal evidence.

Evidence state: PX-1 DOW article remains plaintiff's strongest source for ground-based extraction force in Caracas plus Maduro out of power and U.S. oversight until transition. PX-2 White House SAP and DX-1/DX-2 are adverse on operation framing: targeted/limited, law enforcement, apprehension/transport, about two hours on ground, not invasion, not extended operation, nearly 200 Americans downtown Caracas grabbing indicted individual. PX-3 is contextual support for U.S. control/objective context but less decisive.

Closing theory: Entry and combat-capacity elements are effectively conceded/proven. Defense's strongest point is edge case: capture without territorial occupation is NO. Plaintiff response: the record shows more than capture; official DOW/White House source says Maduro was out of power, U.S. would oversee Venezuela until safe transition, and successor was willing to do what U.S. thought necessary. The proposition does not require a permanent invasion or extended occupation; it requires qualifying objective. By preponderance, ground entry plus removal from power plus U.S. transition oversight satisfies removing sitting government/operational control, despite law-enforcement labels. Acknowledge weakness candidly: if council treats two-hour capture/transport label as conclusive, plaintiff loses; but source hierarchy and complete context favor YES.

Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing opportunity: defendant, `closings:defendant`; final act allowed `deliver_closing_statement`; no evidence submission allowed.

Final scan: `get_case` and `list_evidence` completed. Plaintiff closing filed and no new evidence appeared. Evidence list remains five text-readable items: PX-1 Department of War article, PX-2 White House SAP, PX-3 White House oil-revenue fact sheet, DX-1 White House Rubio release, DX-2 Department of War Hegseth transcript. Exact evidence contents were already read during arguments; no new byte-sensitive issue requiring further reads.

Plaintiff closing theory: entry, combat capacity, and timing are established; objective is the only serious dispute. Plaintiff relies on PX-1's statements that Maduro was out of power, U.S. would oversee Venezuela until transition, and successor would work with U.S.; plaintiff argues capture was means, removal/transition oversight was objective.

Defense closing theory: concede physical entry and combat-capable raid to focus on objective. Plaintiff still fails the qualifying objective required by proposition. The most precise objective sources are PX-2/DX-1/DX-2: law-enforcement operation to apprehend and transport Maduro/Flores for prosecution; forces on ground about two hours; not a war against Venezuela; not an invasion; not an extended military operation; raid/capture/arrest; grabbed an indicted individual in support of law enforcement. PX-1's adverse language is political aftermath, not proof of ground troops seizing/holding territory or exercising operational control over Caracas. The proposition's edge case would be meaningless if any head-of-state capture causing loss of power automatically counted. Rodriguez being sworn in cuts against U.S. ground control. PX-3 is financial/diplomatic leverage, not ground-troop operational control. No admitted UN record changes the source hierarchy. Close for NO on failure of objective element.
